segments.text: So, the class today is introduction to natural language processing.

But in addition to natural language understanding, there's one major other segment of NLP. Does anyone have an idea what that might be?

We often have a dichotomy between two major segments, natural language understanding and natural language generation. Yeah, exactly. So I would say that's almost perfect if he had said understand and generate. So very good.
So I say natural link technology to handle human language usually text using computers to aid human machine communication.
And this can include things like question answering dialogue or generation of code that can be executed with computers.
It can also aid human human communication and this can include things like machine translation or spell checking or assisted writing.
And then a final segment that people might think about a little bit less is analyzing and understanding a language.
And this includes things like syntactic analysis, text classification, entity recognition and linking.

And these can be used for various reasons, not necessarily for direct human machine communication, but also for like aggregating information across large things for scientific studies and other things like that. I'll give a few examples.
We now use NLP many times a day, sometimes without even knowing it. So whenever you're typing a doc in Google docs, there's, you know, spell checking and grammar checking going on behind.

It's gotten frightened and frighteningly good recently that where it checks like most of my mistakes and rarely flags things that are not mistakes. So obviously they have powerful models running behind that.
